Celebrate open-source software , hardware , and open data through engaging talks , hands-on workshops , and friendly networking . Whether you’re a seasoned contributor or new to FOSS, LucknowFOSS 2.0 welcomes everyone to share ideas , collaborate , and explore cutting-edge innovations . Why Attend? Engaging Talks All things FOSS Community-Driven Networking Stay tuned for venue and ticket announcements (with student discounts). Don’t miss this chance to connect with fellow enthusiasts, discover new projects, and shape the future of FOSS in India. chunk_text:
BarCamp Bangalore – BENGALURU
Organiser: FOSS Event Grants
Type: Other
Date: Sat, 18 Apr 2026 00:00:00 -0000
URL: https://fossunited.org/grants/events?s=BarCamp Bangalore

BarCamp Bangalore Type: Other Chapter: FOSS Event Grants Location: BENGALURU Start: 18 April 2026, 12:00 AM End: 18 April 2026, 12:00 AM ⭐ Must Attend Event BarCamp Bangalore is an open, volunteer-led, participant-driven unconference where attendees propose and run sessions themselves. It celebrates peer-to-peer learning, spontaneous discussions, and community-led knowledge sharing across a wide range of topics rather than a fixed agenda.

We are planning a full-day unconference that brings together a diverse and engaged audience including software professionals, entrepreneurs, students, professors, venture capitalists and angel investors, photographers, social-change activists, NGO policy makers, bloggers, consultants, reporters, and more. Over the years BarCamp Bangalore has attracted thousands of participants, hundreds of session proposals, and extensive community engagement both in person and online.

The event will offer multiple parallel session tracks where participants can share ideas, discuss real-world challenges, showcase projects, lead workshops, and network with other passionate individuals. It is a unique opportunity for people to connect, learn, collaborate and co-create in an inclusive environment that encourages experimentation, creativity, and community building. View Event Details →
title: BarCamp Bangalore – BENGALURU

chunk_text:
Linux Installation Party – TBA
Organiser: National Institute of Technology(NIT) Srinagar-FOSS Club
Type: Linux Installation Party
Date: Sat, 18 Apr 2026 10:30:00 -0000
URL: https://fossunited.org/c/national-institute-of-technology-srinagar/linux-installation-party

Linux Installation Party Type: Linux Installation Party Chapter: National Institute of Technology(NIT) Srinagar-FOSS Club Location: TBA Start: 18 April 2026, 10:30 AM End: 18 April 2026, 02:00 PM Curious about Linux but nervous about installing it? Tired of forced updates and slow performance? Join us for our free Linux Installation Party! Our team and friendly volunteers will help you safely install a beginner-friendly Linux distribution (like Ubuntu or Mint) on your machine, step-by-step. No prior tech experience is needed—if you know how to use a computer, you're ready! 🛠️ What to Expect : Expert Help : 1-on-1 guidance through the entire installation and setup process. Ready-to-Go : We provide the bootable USB drives with the best Linux "flavors." Community : Meet fellow tech enthusiasts and open-source advocates (plus, snacks!). 🎒 What to Bring : The laptop or PC you want to install Linux on, plus the power adapter/charger. CRITICAL : Please back up all your important data to an external drive or the cloud before you arrive. (Optional) A blank 8GB+ USB drive if you want to take a Linux installer home. More details will be shared soon! View Event Details →
title: Linux Installation Party – TBA

About IndiaFOSS
IndiaFOSS is an annual Free and Open Source Software (FOSS) conference + unconference, organised with passion and commitment by the FOSS United community. It's not just a gathering; it's a celebration of software innovation and the people who make it happen. Whether you are a seasoned developer, an aspiring enthusiast, or someone with a passion for technology, IndiaFOSS invites you to be part of this vibrant community.

chunk_text:
India's Largest FOSS Hackathon
March 2026 | Hybrid
FOSS Hack is back for its sixth edition! With the month-long hybrid hackathon,
we bring together students, professionals and communities to contribute better
to Free and Open Source software projects. Dive right in!
Go to Event Page
Project Ideas
View all Projects
₹ 5,00,000
Prize pool
8
Local hosts
5430
Participants
2357
Teams
788
Projects
Registrations Closed
Timeline
08 March
Registration
Register now to express your interest in participating!
If you are interested in hacking at a localhost, hurry up, because limited spots!
1-31 March
Create. Contribute. Hack
Make progress towards your project,
whether it's a project you're building or an existing project you're contributing to,
whether you're coding, designing, documenting, testing, etc.
During this time,
you can also seek out mentorship and expert advice regarding your work!
31 March
Last Call
Wrap up your work in a neat bow - make sure that your project
has an appropriate description and a demo video.
If you're writing code,
make sure to add a link to the source code repository, e.g., GitHub repo.
4 May
Results
After multiple rounds of elimination,
we will be sharing the results on Monday, the 4th of May. Projects will be evaluated
for novelty, completeness, and relevance. For additional details, please see FAQs.
Partner Projects Programme
An effort to provide FOSS Hack participants with time and mentorship to be able to contribute to curated open-source projects.
FOSSEE
Rethink DNS
Zasper
Pyodide
Maxun
XenevaOS
OpenAlgo
View All
It’s not just code...
Documentation
Help improve documentation for open-source projects — write guides,
tutorials, READMEs, or API references.
Data
Contribute datasets (OSMaps), data pipelines, or data analysis
to open-source projects.
Design
Improve the user experience and visual design of open-source projects —
create logos, UI/UX improvements, or design systems.
Code
Solve issues and make PRs contribution to OSS repositories.
Why Participate?
Kickstart your journey into the FOSS ecosystem.
Figure out how you can contribute to the Digital Commons
Access meaningful mentorship and support as you try to solve real-world problems.
All this with a chance to win upto 5 Lakh INR in cash prizes :)
Rules in a nutshell
Evaluation based on contributions made during the event duration
Core functionality shouldn't depend on closed-source software or closed/proprietary APIs
Your project must have a valid FOSS license
The cash prize will be split among the winners at the discretion of the jury
No blockchain/web3 or crypto projects.
Why?
Check out the
Rules & FAQ's
page for more details
Your projects will be judged on the basis of how meaningful of a problem you've decided to work on, and how well described that problem is. We will also look at the projects to evaluate how well you understand the codebase. If you have used an LLM to generate code, please make sure to give necessary attributions.
Mentors
Adwaith Shan
Cybersecurit
title: FOSS Hack 2026

Growing India's
FOSS Ecosystem
Explore FOSS Events
Join Forum
ABOUT
FOSS United is a non-profit foundation that aims at promoting & strengthening the Free and Open Source Software (FOSS) ecosystem in India.
󠀠
GOALS
01
To promote the spirit of hacking and tinkering.
GOALS
02
To build quality FOSS for public good.
GOALS
03
To evangelise the use of FOSS in different sectors.
Programs
City Communities
Join a community in your city and take part in the FOSS Movement.
View More
FOSS Clubs
Explore our initiative that promotes FOSS culture among students.
View More
FOSS for Social Good
Know more about OASIS coalition that promotes usage of FOSS in social sector.
View More
Flagship Events
IndiaFOSS
IndiaFOSS is an annual FOSS conference, organised with passion and commitment by the FOSS
United community.
View More
FOSS Hack
FOSS Hack is our flagship hackathon to promote FOSS by bringing enthusiasts together to
build or extend FOSS projects.

Industry Partnership
The foundation was started as a collaboration between the teams at Frappe, the publisher of ERPNext, one of the largest FOSS projects out of India, and Zerodha. Currently we have two more partners supporting us, and we invite more organisations to join the movement.
